title: Introduction to Vue.js
content: ### What is Vue.js? Vue.js is a **progressive JavaScript framework** for building user interfaces. It is designed to be incrementally adoptable — you can use it for a small part of a page or scale it up to build full single-page applications (SPAs). ### Progressive Framework Unlike monolithic frameworks, Vue is built from the ground up to be flexible: - **Library mode**: Drop Vue into any existing page via a CDN script tag - **Framework mode**: Use the full toolchain with build tools, routing, and state management ### Virtual DOM Vue uses a **Virtual DOM** to optimize rendering. Instead of directly manipulating the browser DOM on every change, Vue creates a lightweight JavaScript representation of the UI, computes the difference (diffing), and applies only the necessary updates to the real DOM. ### Reactivity System Vue's reactivity system tracks dependencies and automatically updates the DOM when data changes. In Vue 3, this is powered by JavaScript Proxies, which intercept property access and mutation. ### Comparison with React and Angular | Feature | Vue | React | Angular | |---|---|---|---| | Type | Progressive framework | UI library | Full framework | | Template | HTML-based (.vue) | JSX | HTML-based (.html) | | Learning Curve | Low | Moderate | High | | State Management | Pinia/Vuex | Redux/Zustand | NgRx/Service | | Bundle Size | ~33 KB gzipped | ~42 KB gzipped | ~143 KB gzipped | ### Single-File Components (SFC) Vue components are written in `.vue` files, which encapsulate template, script, and styles in one file: ```vue ```

quiz.question: What does Vue.js use to optimize DOM updates?
